media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again

characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: The Ace: There doesn't seem to be anything that the Friz is explicitly bad at. She has extensive knowledge in just about every science, including astronomy, biology, chemistry, zoology, and meteorology. The only time she's ever shown in any sort of trouble is when she overexerts herself in an athletic event (used to show the effects of fatigue on the human body), and even then, she still wins. Affectionate Nickname: She's frequently referred to as "the Friz" by her students. Ambiguously Human: While she seems human, she is several times implied to beOlder Than She Looks. This, and the fact that she has access toMagitekfar more advanced than anything else in the setting, has led to quite a bit ofWild Mass Guessingabout her true nature. Ballet: Those dazzling figure skating moves she shows off on the frictionless baseball field at one point in "Plays Ball" had to come fromsomewhere. Berserk Button: Apparentlyshe's the only one allowed to make gross-out puns, especially when they involve nasal mucus. Luckily, she'll just give you anicy stareof disapprovalif you make those puns instead.

characters.name: Ms. Valerie Frizzle
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: The Ace: There doesn't seem to be anything that the Friz is explicitly bad at. She has extensive knowledge in just about every science, including astronomy, biology, chemistry, zoology, and meteorology. The only time she's ever shown in any sort of trouble is when she overexerts herself in an athletic event (used to show the effects of fatigue on the human body), and even then, she still wins. Affectionate Nickname: She's frequently referred to as "the Friz" by her students. Ambiguously Human: While she seems human, she is several times implied to beOlder Than She Looks. This, and the fact that she has access toMagitekfar more advanced than anything else in the setting, has led to quite a bit ofWild Mass Guessingabout her true nature. Ballet: Those dazzling figure skating moves she shows off on the frictionless baseball field at one point in "Plays Ball" had to come fromsomewhere. Berserk Button: Apparentlyshe's the only one allowed to make gross-out puns, especially when they involve nasal mucus. Luckily, she'll just give you anicy stareof disapprovalif you make those puns instead.

characters.name: Arnold Perlstein
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Adaptational Intelligence: He knows a lot about geology to the point where he's the youngest member of the GRANITE society, but in the original books, the best he can manage in terms of geology is a styrofoam peanut made up to look like a rock, even as his classmates are able to find actual rocks around him while they're going through the inside of the Earth. Adaptation Dye-Job: He was blond in the original books, but a redhead in the series. Adorably Precocious Child: He is an 8 year old kid who knows enough about rocks to earn a high award on it. Affectionate Nickname: Molly Cule calls him "Sugar" throughout "The Magic School Bus Meets Molly Cule". Badass Adorable: He certainly has his moments of outstanding bravery.

characters.name: Carlos Ramon
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Actually Pretty Funny: He sometimes gets this reaction from the rest of the class, even when they do the usual collective groan of his name. Big Brother Instinct: Is very protective of his classmates and his brother Mikey. He also believes in his brother. Borrowed Catchphrase: In "In the Arctic", he borrows Ralphie's "Is it just me, or...?" catchphrase when the heat source that just appeared smells fishy. Canon Immigrant: While Carlos was in the cartoon from the start, he wasn't a character in the original books untilIn The Time of the Dinosaurs, which was published two days before the animated series premiered. Class Clown: Is a jokester and in a class.

characters.name: Dorothy Ann Hudson
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Adaptational Costume Change: In the original book series, her regular outfit consists of a yellow shirt with a pink plaid skirt. For the cartoon, her shirt becomes purple, and her skirt becomes plain blue. Let's face it, animating a plaid skirt on a TV budget would be asking a lot. All There in the Manual: Her last name is revealed inMeet the Class, aRides Againtie-in book. Berserk Button:Do not doubt for one second the nutritional value of broccoli.Dorothy Ann:According to my research, broccoli is averyhealthy vegetable!Donotbelittle math in front of her. Do not doubt for one second the nutritional value of broccoli.Dorothy Ann:According to my research, broccoli is averyhealthy vegetable! Donotbelittle math in front of her.

characters.name: Keesha Franklin
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Agent Scully: Keesha is always skeptical of anything unscientific. Arbitrary Skepticism: ...except a bus that can transform, shrink, etc. Course, sheisone ofMs. Frizzle's students, so she sees the bus do things like that on a regular basis. Bad "Bad Acting": In "The Magnetic Mambo", her dancing style reeks of this when the Friz kids are preparing for the Walkerville Elementary talent show, despite her (admittedly seldom referenced) ballet background. Ballet: Mentions having lessons in "Wet All Over". She's also seen doing an arabesque on Mercury in "Gets Lost in Space". Of course, as seen in "Plays Ball", it doesn't help her one whistle in a world without friction. Berserk Button: Using her friends in malicious schemes will result in her doing everything in her power to humiliate you, as seen in her tricking Janet in "Gets a Bright Idea."

characters.name: Phoebe Terese
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Beware the Nice Ones: Just ask Carlos, Ralphie, Tim, and Janet. Birds of a Feather: Hinted at with Arnold. Catchphrase: "At my old school, we never did things like this." This originated in the cartoon before being transplanted into the books starting withThe Magic School Bus In the Time of the Dinosaurs. Color-Coded Characters: Red. Daddy's Girl: Implied in "Going Batty."

characters.name: Ralph "Ralphie" Tennelli
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Academic Athlete: Was put in charge of coming up with an idea for Broadcast Day, and was horrified when his mom told him to stay at home. Also reads a book that DA recommended to him. Acrofatic: Although it isn't really dwelled on, he is clearly meant to be "chubby" in contrast to his "skinny" classmates (best demonstrated in "Goes To Mussel Beach", where all four boys are seen in their bathing suits and he is the only one with a prominent gut) but is also the class athlete. Actually Pretty Funny: Though just by a small margin, he found more of Carlos's puns funny than their classmates did. Agent Mulder: In contrast toKeesha, he jumps to outlandish conclusions more than once, such as when he was the first to believe that Ms. Frizzle was a vampire. The All-American Boy: Check out his outfit, his interest in sports, love for food, and his mechanical expertise.Subverted with his full name, which is three-quarters Italian.

characters.name: Tim Wright
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: All There in the Manual: His last name is revealed inMeet the Class, aRides Againtie-in book. Catchphrase: "We've been frizzled." Color-Coded Characters: Blue in the Original series. Teal in Rides Again. Crouching Moron, Hidden Badass: In the spider episode, he saves Phoebe and Keesha from an orb spider by cutting the web lines they were attached to, and does tug-of-war with agiant spiderto save Ms. Frizzle from getting eaten by it. Deadpan Snarker: Has a moment of this when he comments, "This is one weird house," in the Haunted House episode.Ralphie:You mean your house doesn't have a jungle, a city, an ocean, and an alpine wonderland?Tim(cheerily)Nah. The landlord wouldn't go for it.

characters.name: Wanda Li
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Adaptational Costume Change: In the books that predate the TV series, Wanda's default outfit consists of red overalls worn over a pink shirt. In the TV series, this gives way to a red vest and pants combo worn over a pink shirt. Starting withDinosaurs, her outfit in the books is changed to match the TV series. Ballet: Downplayed a lot compared to Keesha. She might not have had any formal training, but she's a self-admitted fan, and she shows she might be self-taught early on in "Holiday Special" when she briefly imitates the movements of the dancers she anticipates seeing onstage that night. Berserk Button: Accusing her of gluttony, as Arnold does during the climax of "For Lunch", is a surefire way to get a reaction out of her. Borrowed Catchphrase: In "Wet All Over", she directs Ralphie's "Is it just me, or...?" catchphrase at him. Catchphrase:"Come on, ya buncha weaselly wimps!""What are we gonna do, what are we gonna do, what are we gonna dooooo?!""Yawn burger with a side of snores."

characters.name: Liz
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Animal Gender-Bender: She sports three horns on her head, which only male Jackson's chameleons have. In real life, females typically have no horns or traces of the rostral (nose) horn. Animals Not to Scale: A normal Jackson's chameleon could fit in an adult human's hand. Liz is roughly the size of a cat. Anthropomorphic Shift: In the original book series, Liz is mostly just a normal lizard. In the TV series, she looks more anthropomorphic and is capable of tasks like driving the bus. In some of the computer games, she can talk. Balloon Belly: Liz, youknewyou would put on pounds after eating all those cakes in the bakery. Big Eater: Will eatallthe cakes in the bakery if she feels like it!

characters.name: The Bus
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Animate Inanimate Object: It has a face that emotes, though no one seems to notice (or they've just grown so used to the weirdness that they ignore it). Also, it candrive itself. Cool Car: Admit it: you wish you had a bus like this, don't you? De-power: It loses its magic near the end of "In The Zone" and turns into an ordinary bus. Luckily Jyoti is able to help get its magic back. Disney Death: It has two of them. Both during Seasonal Episodes.In "Holiday special", it hits itself with the unrecycling ray and turns into a pile of cans and bottles. Luckily Miss Frizzle, Murph and the class are able to re-recycle them and bring the bus back."In The Zone" takes this up to eleven when the bus starts glitching out due to getting new magicware which makes it unresponsive. Luckily Ms Frizzle, Maven and the class get it back to normal so it can get back home. Only for it to not make it in time and lose its magic, becoming a regular bus. Jyoti manages to save the day by using the geographical time zone to help bring its magic back. In "Holiday special", it hits itself with the unrecycling ray and turns into a pile of cans and bottles. Luckily Miss Frizzle, Murph and the class are able to re-recycle them and bring the bus back.

characters.name: Janet Perlstein
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Aesop Amnesia: She learns to not be such a rude person in nearly every single episode she appears in, but it never sticks. Alpha Bitch: While not as manipulative as most examples, she tends to be quite mean. Big Bad: The closest thing to one the show has— whenever she shows up in an episode, you can bet that she'll make something go horribly wrong. Though it's downplayed as she doesn't alwaysintendto... she just usually wants to show off. Body Horror: She's revealed to have an ingrown toenail in "Janet's Mystery Gene", much to the class's horror. According to Arnold, it runs in the family. Breakout Character: Downplayed, since she never becomes a full regular, but nevertheless, Janet was a one-off character in the original books, appearing only inThe Magic School Bus Lost in the Solar System. Perhaps because that's the book that was adapted as the series pilot, she became a recurring character on the show. She also appeared in that book's video game adaptation,The Magic School Bus Explores the Solar System, possibly making her the only non-regular to make it into the game series.

characters.name: Lenny the Lifeguard
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Adapted Out: Lenny never appears in the TV series or in any other media outside of the original books. The Bus Came Back: He returns for a cameo inThe Magic School Bus and the Climate Challenge. This is his only appearance outside ofOcean Floor. Muscle Beach Bum: He has the physique, and we first see him trying to impress everyone on the beach with his previous rescues. He doesn't wear a speedo, though. Small Name, Big Ego: He spends all ofOcean Floorboasting about how he's rescuing the class, but he's actually just a useless tagalong on their magical field trip. When they return to the beach at the end, he takes credit for saving them, calling it "the greatest rescue of my career."

characters.name: Mikey Ramon
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Cheerful Child: Ms. Frizzle is delighted by Mikey, and enamored with his enthusiasm. The other kids think that he's a tad out of touch with reality. Crazy Enough to Work: Mikey operates on this principle regularly. Crazy-Prepared: His wheelchair comes prepared with a raft in the ferris wheel episode that was able to hold himself and eight classmates. Foil: To Janet. Both are really smart and both have a family member in Ms. Frizzle's class. But whereas Janet is aJerkassandInsufferable Genius, Mikey is much more humble and friendly. Genius Cripple: This is a kid capable of programming tasks on an 1990s computer.

characters.name: Mr. Ruhle
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Adaptational Personality Change: In the original show, he is shown to be a stern but fair principal, but inRides Again, he noticeably acts more mellowed out. Agent Scully: Subverted. While he appears to be skeptical of D.A.'s story for much of "Cracks a Yolk", it's all a guilt-tripping ruse. Catchphrase: "Thought you could fool old Ruhle, eh?" Evil Laugh: Gives one when he reminds Frizzle about their bet about the pinball machine. Hidden Depths: He knows a lot about birds.

characters.name: Harry Arm
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Ironic Name: His arms aren'thairydespitehis name. Misunderstood Loner with a Heart of Gold: In his first appearance, he appears to be a bit of a bully, but when he next appears, he showshe was simply leading Arnold into a science lesson he had himself learned from Ms. Frizzle. Shut Up, Hannibal!: Gives one to Janet in "Works Out" when she threatens to have Ms. Frizzle disqualified for taking too long between events. Stealth Mentor: Revealed to be one to Arnold at the end of "In the Arctic".

characters.name: Carmen
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: All There in the Manual: Her name is only said in the original books. Always Someone Better: When the schoolkids make elemental-themed science projects to be put on a rocket thatNASAhopes aliens will discover, Ms. Frizzle's students feel that Carmen's rock-themed rock project is far better than their air-based project. Demoted to Extra: She was in Ms. Frizzle's class in the original books, but in the animated series she makes a single appearance, as a student from a different home room, in "Goes on Air". This also happened to her along with the students who weren't in the cartoon in the books, being reduced in prominence in favor of the eight from the cartoon. Nice Girl: Carmen is a smiling girl who is proud of her project without being a braggart and also shows interest in her peers' less impressive project. Pink Means Feminine: Carmen comes across as good-natured and curious about her friends' projects, and her whole wardrobe (shirt, skirt, shoes, suspenders and socks) is in varying shades of pink.

characters.name: The Producers
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Ambiguously Related: Both producers share similar characteristics designs and race backgrounds as Tim and Wanda, but its unknown if they're related to the two or not. Death Glare: The female producer, any time the male producer forgets he's not the only producer. The Friends Who Never Hang:Both producers hardly interact with each other with the exceptions of "Gets Ants in its Pants" and "Kicks Up A Storm".Outside of Liz, neither of the producers interact with the main cast onscreen. Only the male producer interacted with Arnold via phone in "Makes A Stink" Both producers hardly interact with each other with the exceptions of "Gets Ants in its Pants" and "Kicks Up A Storm". Outside of Liz, neither of the producers interact with the main cast onscreen. Only the male producer interacted with Arnold via phone in "Makes A Stink"

characters.name: Ms. Fiona Frizzle
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Alliterative Name:FionaFrizzle. Big Entrance: Like her sister before her, she shows up in various episodes in different ways. Borrowed Catchphrase: She uses all the catchphrases that the original Miss Frizzle used when teaching the class. Of course, taking their relationship into account, it might be a family thing. Contrasting Sequel Protagonist: Downplayed. She actually hasa lotin common with her sister, Valerie, in her teaching methods and even character. The one major difference that sets these two apart is that personality-wise, Fiona is more laid-back and behaves like aCool Big Sisto the kids whereas Valerie was moremotherlytowards them in the original series. Makes sense as Fiona is the younger one of the two sisters. Cool Big Sis: Fiona's dynamic with the class is more similar to this than Valerie's style of teaching, which is more maternal.

characters.name: Jyoti Kaur
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Berserk Button:Leave her hair alone.A magical ever-growing plant learned that the hard way in the very first episode ofRides Again. Big Damn Heroes: In "Kids In Space", she's the one who rescues Wanda by using one of her inventions to pull her back. Bollywood Nerd: She is a girl of Indian descent and is also real technology expert. Character Tics: She has a habit of starting her dialogue with a single-word sentence, before continuing with her thoughts. Color-Coded Characters: Purple.

characters.name: The Friz
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Beauty, Brains, and Brawn: The beauty to Fiona's brawn and Frizzle's brains. She lacks intelligence and muscle but has plenty of humor in her. Brainless Beauty: And ratherunkemptabout it, too. Cloud Cuckoolander: The wackiest of the Fragments.

characters.name: Fiona
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Action Girl: Fitting with her daring personaltiy and sense of adventure. Beauty, Brains, and Brawn: The brawn to the Friz's beauty and Frizzle's brains. Her sense of adventure is stronger by itself, and she shows some admittedly subtle muscle compared to the other Fragments. DreamWorks Face: She does this on Netflix's official icon for "The Frizz Connection". Genius Bruiser: She's the brave tough side of Fiona but is shown to be just as smart as Frizzle. Tank-Top Tomboy: She wears a purple tank top.

characters.name: Frizzle
characters.media_title: The Magic School Bus Rides Again
characters.description: Beauty, Brains, and Brawn: The brains to the Friz's beauty and Fiona's brawn. Unlike the other two, she's both cautious and calculating. Catchphrase: "Safety first!" Grammar Nazi: Only once, but when Arnold says they have to get to the other Frizzles quick, she corrects Arnold by saying "Quickly! Adverb." Insistent Terminology: Since they're doing their work on a hot air balloon and not at home, it's "balloonwork", not "homework", and Frizzle will let out a stage "ahem" anytime someone refers to it as the latter and not the former. Prim and Proper Bun: Being the stuffiest of the Fragments, she's got her hair tied up this way.
